title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, June 1885, No. 9
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: I. That on the 20th of the month of July, being at that time in or about Latitude 45 N. and betwixt and between Longitude 51 W. and 51.10° W., so near as could be made out, the captain of the steamboat "Glory of the Morning Star" (chartered _for this occasion only_ by the Government of the Republic, without any damage, precedent or future lien whatsoever), by name James Murphy, of Cork, Ireland, and domiciled within the aforesaid terms, boundaries, etc., did in a loud voice at about
quoteLast: , when it was already light, cry out "That's Hur," or words to that effect.
title: On Something
author: Belloc, Hilaire, 1870-1953

quoteLast: , we saw another burying-place called Kubt el Kaireen: we arrived about five in the evening at the gate of Hamah.
title: Travels of Lady Hester Stanhope, Volume 2 (of 3)
